summary: Set 015 Session 2 (dabbler-access-harvester migration to canonical Option B): executed the 16-step clone-and-swap recipe from Session 1's ai-assignment.md. End state: harvester at C:\Users\denmi\source\repos\dabbler-access-harvester\ is a normal git repo at canonical Option B layout (.git/ is a directory, source files at root, no .bare/, no main/ subdirectory), on branch migrate/dabbler-ai-router-pip tracking origin, working tree clean. The .venv/ has dabbler-ai-router-0.1.1 cleanly installed; pip metadata and import-time __version__ both report 0.1.1, resolving the pre-migration discrepancy. Old container preserved at C:\Users\denmi\source\repos\dabbler-access-harvester-old\ for ~1 week as rollback safety net. Two bundle files at C:\Users\denmi\source\repos\ preserve the active migration branch and the PoC branch (harvester-migrate-pip-20260506-132426.bundle and harvester-vba-poc-20260506-132426.bundle). Per operator decision (b), the worktree-vba-symbol-resolution-session-1 PoC branch was NOT carried into the new repo - the bundle preserves its 3 unique commits indefinitely. Operator-confirmed VS Code smoke test green: extension activates without manifest-registration errors, Cost Dashboard renders, Session Set Explorer shows expected session sets bucketed correctly. Notable gap surfaced during execution: python -m ai_router.worktree fails in the new repo's venv because PyPI's dabbler-ai-router 0.1.1 predates Set 017's worktree-CLI shipment; validation fell back to raw git worktree list plus filesystem signal checks (all four canonical signals passed). Filed as backlog: bump dabbler-ai-router PyPI release once orchestration repo is ready. No code changes in dabbler-ai-orchestration; this session's commit captures only Session 2's close-out artifacts. Old container + bundles cleanup checklist (~1 week from 2026-05-06) recorded in change-log.md. Session 3 (platform pip upgrade) remains; same orchestrator continues for execution continuity.

reason.specifics: Session 3 (platform pip upgrade + smoke test) executes the platform recipe authored in Session 1's ai-assignment.md. Same orchestrator continues for execution continuity; the platform plan is concrete and the migration is dramatically simpler than Session 2's (no clone-and-swap needed because platform is already at canonical Option B-compatible layout).
